title: Unified Network Operations Dashboard
summary: Single source of truth across 130+ facilities, 5 sub-brands, and 3 funds. SiteLink revenue + GoHighLevel leads + QuickBooks ledger + Juniper Square investor data, normalized into one operational view.

steps.description: SiteLink API access via Brian + read-only ETL into a Postgres warehouse on Supabase. Schema: facilities, units, rentals, vacancies.
GoHighLevel lead funnel sync — pipeline stages, per-facility routing, lead-source attribution.
QuickBooks Online + Stessa revenue recognition tie-out. Sync to facility-level revenue rollup.
Dashboard MVP shipped: Network KPIs, top/bottom leaderboard, brand-mix view, alert engine.
Per-facility drill-down: occupancy curve, climate-control mix, RV/boat utilization, lead funnel per location.
Alert rules engine — declining occupancy, lead SLA breach, sync lag, CPL spike — wired to Slack + email.
Brand portfolio view — split-out for 10F Storage, Storage Depot, Big Guy, Carolina Secure, Self Storage Max.
Investor-facing view: read-only dashboard slice for LPs, gated through Juniper Square SSO.
Mobile-responsive layout. Capranos + Oakley check facility status from anywhere.
Production hardening: monitoring, alerting, daily ETL health checks, on-call runbook.

title: Marketing Landing-Page Engine
summary: Template-driven facility, market, and brand landing pages — every page SEO-targeted for local-pack searches. Per-facility deploy in hours, not weeks.

steps.description: Audit current site templates and information architecture. Inventory facility content gaps across 130 properties.
Next.js + Vercel rebuild scaffold. Per-facility static generation, ISR for content updates.
Facility detail page template — hero map, unit-mix selector, climate-control filter, rate display from SiteLink, schema.org LocalBusiness markup.
Market hub pages (city + metro level). "Storage in Raleigh" / "Storage in Houston" type pages targeting metro-level intent.
Brand landing pages — distinct identity per sub-brand. Storage Depot, Big Guy, Carolina Secure, Self Storage Max each get their own brand homepage.
Conversion components: rent-now flow, contactless move-in CTA, hero image preconnect, mobile-first design.
SEO: structured data, sitemap automation, internal linking optimization, Core Web Vitals pass — fix the INP and TTFB issues from the Day 1 Backlog.
Per-facility content CMS — facility manager can update hours, amenities, photos without engineering.
A/B test framework — hero copy, CTA variants, climate-control upsell. Wire to GoHighLevel attribution.
New-facility playbook: launch a new acquisition's facility page in 4 hours from acquisition close.

title: Internal Tools Triage
summary: Audit the top spreadsheets eating staff time across accounting, ops, marketing, investments, and construction. Replace the top 3 with purpose-built tools.

steps.description: Interview Trent, Cliff, Brad, and team — list every spreadsheet that's reviewed weekly. Score by hours-per-week × seniority of editor.
Pick top 3 by ROI. Likely candidates: (a) acquisition pipeline tracker, (b) fund distribution calculator, (c) facility maintenance log.
Tool 1 — acquisition pipeline. Replace spreadsheet with web app, Juniper Square sync, automated reminders.
Tool 2 — distribution calculator. Pull NOI from QuickBooks, apply waterfall, generate LP statements automatically.
Tool 3 — facility maintenance log. SiteLink-linked, mobile-friendly for on-site managers, photo upload, vendor dispatch.
Permission model — role-based access aligned to org structure.
Audit logs + change history. Every edit traceable for compliance.
Migrate existing spreadsheet data. Archive sheets read-only.
Train teams. Office hours every Friday for two weeks.
Measure: hours saved per week, error rate before/after. Pick next 3 to replace in Phase 2.

title: Unified Customer Portal Layer
summary: Today the flagship gets a white-labeled TenantConnect domain; the other 4 sub-brands show *.tenantconnect.com. Fix the inconsistency, fix the broken chat, establish the standard for every future acquisition.

steps.description: TenantConnect contract review — confirm white-label terms cover all 5 sub-brands. Resolve commercial blockers.
CNAME setup: portal.storagedepot.com, portal.bigguystorage.com, portal.carolinasecurestorage.com, portal.selfstoragemax.com.
Audit TenantConnect chat widget config across all 5 portals. Fix broken widget ID / DNS / chat provider misconfiguration.
Default the portal landing to "Existing Customers." Move Auction Buyer + Vendor/Employee links to secondary menu.
Brand-specific theming per portal — distinct logo, color, hero image while sharing the TenantConnect backend.
Customer-portal analytics: portal usage, payment success, chat resolution time. Wire to Mixpanel + Slack.
Define the "new acquisition portal onboarding" runbook — every new portfolio plugs into the unified portal stack on close day.
Self-service workflows: late-payment plan setup, unit transfer requests, gate code reset — all without phone calls.
Inbound call deflection metrics — measure (910) call volume before / after. Target -30% within 90 days.
Phase 2 prep: evaluate replacing TenantConnect with a custom portal if the per-tenant TC fees justify the build cost.

title: AI Agent Workforce
summary: Per-department AI agents that take real action — not chatbots. Each agent owns a workflow, has tools, and reports outcomes. Aligned to Christopher Taylor's industry-first AI mandate.

steps.description: Agent infrastructure: orchestrator, tool registry, observability (Langfuse), eval framework. Anthropic Claude as base; OpenAI for fallback.
Accounting agent — Trent's helper. Reconciles SiteLink revenue to QuickBooks daily, flags variances, drafts journal entries for review.
Operations agent — Capranos's helper. Monitors per-facility KPIs, drafts daily ops report, escalates declining facilities.
Marketing agent — drafts paid ad creative variations per market, A/B test reports, lead-to-rental funnel diagnostics.
Investments agent — Cliff's helper. Drafts LOI summaries, pro forma comparisons, capital deployment recommendations.
Construction agent — Cliff's other helper. Procore + ClickUp sync, milestone risk detection, capex pacing analysis.
Human-in-the-loop UI — every agent action queued for review by the relevant owner before execution. No autonomous side effects until trust is built.
MCP server exposing internal data to agents — facility data, fund data, construction data, with audit logging.
Agent eval suite — does the accounting agent reconcile correctly? Run nightly against historical data, alert on regressions.
Cost monitoring — token spend per agent per day. Cap budgets; alert on anomalies.

title: Construction Project Intelligence Layer
summary: Cliff's construction projects (Dripping Springs, Charlotte, plus pipeline) need real-time pacing visibility. Procore + ClickUp + financial data into one project intelligence view.

steps.description: Procore API integration — pull milestone, RFI, change-order data per active build.
ClickUp task sync — engineering and PM tasks linked to milestones.
Capex burn dashboard — actual vs budget per project, monthly pace, days-to-CO trending.
Schedule risk model — milestone variance triggers cascade analysis to CO date.
Subcontractor performance tracking — change order rate, RFI resolution time, punch list quality.
Pro forma actualization — feed actual lease-up performance back into next-project underwriting.
Investor-facing construction view in Juniper Square — quarterly Class-A build updates.

title: Marketing ↔ Ops Feedback Loop
summary: Close the loop between paid spend, lead generation, conversion, and per-facility occupancy. Today there are blind spots between ad spend and revenue impact.

steps.description: Attribution layer: GoHighLevel lead → SiteLink rental → QuickBooks revenue. UTM-stamped end-to-end.
Per-market spend efficiency: CPL → CPA → LTV by city by brand by channel.
Underperforming facility playbook — when occupancy drops, surface the marketing levers automatically (more paid spend, climate-controlled push, lease incentive).
Climate-controlled upsell engine — based on inventory + local weather + market demand, dynamic recommendation per facility.
Lead routing intelligence — multi-touch, multi-facility, intent-based. SDR equivalent for self-storage.
Quarterly marketing-ops review automation — generates Capranos-ready deck monthly.

title: Fund-Level Reporting Automation
summary: Juniper Square is the LP-facing system of record. Quarterly investor reports, distribution statements, and K-1 prep are still manual. Automate the entire fund-reporting cycle.

steps.description: Juniper Square API integration — read fund metadata, LP positions, distribution history.
Quarterly report generator — fund-level NOI, IRR, distributions paid, deployment status. Auto-drafts the narrative from data.
Per-LP statement builder — capital account, contributions, distributions, current value.
K-1 prep automation — pull from QuickBooks, allocate to LP positions, draft for CPA review.
Capital call workflow — automated draft, DocuSign routing, Plaid-funded settlement.
LP self-service portal — secure access to historical reports, distribution history, document archive.
Fund-level AI agent — Cliff's third helper. Drafts LP comms, summarizes quarterly performance, flags reporting variances.
Opportunistic Offering fundraising automation — drip campaigns, subscription tracking, accredited investor verification.
Fund 4 launch playbook — every system, every report, every workflow productized for the next fund.
Year-1 retrospective. Hours saved across accounting + investments. Cost per LP-touchpoint before / after.
